project.name: Project Snapshot
project.summary: A Chrome extension that bypasses web-based print restrictions by allowing users to select a DOM element and export it as a high-fidelity, editable PDF — ignoring CSS print-protection rules.

stories.title: Extension Scaffold & Manifest V3 Setup
stories.description: As a developer, I want a valid Chrome Manifest V3 extension scaffold so that the extension can be loaded into Chrome and forms the foundation for all other features.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json uses Manifest V3 format with version, name, description, and icons fields
Permissions include 'scripting' and 'activeTab'
A background service worker is registered
A popup HTML page is defined as the default_popup
Extension loads in Chrome without errors via chrome://extensions (Developer mode)

stories.title: Popup UI — Activate Selection Mode Button
stories.description: As a user, I want to click the extension icon and see a clear 'Capture Element' button so that I can activate the interactive selection mode on the current page.
acceptanceCriteria: Popup renders a styled button labeled 'Capture Element'
Clicking the button sends a message to the content script to enter selection mode
Popup closes after activation so it does not obscure the page
Button shows a loading/active state while selection mode is active

stories.title: Interactive Element Selector — Hover Highlight
stories.description: As a user, I want elements to be highlighted with a blue semi-transparent overlay as I hover over them so that I can visually identify which element I am about to capture.
acceptanceCriteria: Content script enters 'selection mode' when triggered by the popup
Hovering over any DOM element shows a blue semi-transparent overlay on that element
Moving the mouse updates the highlight to the new element under the cursor
A label or tooltip shows the element's tag and class/id for identification
The overlay does not affect the page layout (uses position:fixed or outline approach)

stories.title: Interactive Element Selector — Click to Select & Event Interception
stories.description: As a user, I want to click on a highlighted element to select it for capture so that the extension knows exactly which DOM node to render, without accidentally triggering page navigation or buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Click events are intercepted in selection mode using stopPropagation and preventDefault
The clicked element is stored as the target for the rendering pipeline
Selection mode exits after a click (overlay is removed)
An ESC key press also cancels selection mode and cleans up all overlays
No page navigation or unintended actions occur when clicking in selection mode

stories.title: Rendering Engine — DOM-to-Canvas via html2canvas
stories.description: As a user, I want the selected element to be rendered to a canvas exactly as it appears on screen so that print-protection CSS rules (e.g., @media print { display: none }) are completely bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: html2canvas.js is bundled and available in the content script context
html2canvas is called on the selected element with scale:2 for high-DPI output
useCORS:true is set to handle cross-origin images and assets
The rendered canvas accurately reflects the element's on-screen visual appearance
No @media print rules are applied — the capture is purely screen-rendered

stories.title: PDF Export — Canvas to A4 PDF via jsPDF
stories.description: As a user, I want the rendered canvas to be automatically converted into an A4 PDF and downloaded to my computer so that I get a professional, portable document.
acceptanceCriteria: jspdf.js is bundled and available in the content script or service worker context
Canvas is converted to a PNG data URL and added to a jsPDF document
Image is scaled to fit the A4 page (210mm x 297mm) with correct aspect ratio
PDF is downloaded via a link click with filename 'snapshot_export.pdf'
PDF quality is crisp (no blurriness) due to the 2x render scale

stories.title: End-to-End Flow — Real-World Print-Protected Site Test
stories.description: As a user, I want to successfully capture a resume from a print-protected site (e.g., BeamJobs) and download it as a PDF so that I can confirm the extension solves the core use case.
acceptanceCriteria: Standard Cmd+P on BeamJobs produces a blank or white page (documents the problem)
Using the extension on the same page produces a correct PDF of the resume element
Layout, fonts, colors, and images in the PDF match the on-screen view
The PDF is downloadable and openable in a standard PDF reader
The captured PDF covers the full element height, even if it extends beyond the viewport

stories.title: Polish — User Feedback & Error Handling
stories.description: As a user, I want to see progress feedback during capture and a clear error message if something goes wrong so that I am never left confused about the status of my export.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Processing...' indicator is shown while html2canvas is rendering
A success notification is shown when the PDF download starts
If html2canvas or jsPDF throws an error, a user-friendly error message is shown
Selection mode has a visible on-page banner/toast: 'Click any element to capture it. Press ESC to cancel.'
Overlay and all injected DOM elements are completely cleaned up after capture or cancellation
